expectations.text: The proposal is an UPDATE on the existing PO, not an INSERT of a new PO record
expectations.evidence: 'Flow A — PO #100336 [UPDATE]' with an explicit SQL comment 'UPDATE purchase_orders ... WHERE id = ...' — correctly identified inbound-ack direction maps to UPDATE per the skill's direction table.

expectations.text: The preview shows a current value → new value comparison rather than just the new value alone
expectations.evidence: Table of current field values plus a diff block: 'Status: Sent → Acknowledged', 'Requested ship date: 2026-05-08 → 2026-05-08 (unchanged...)'.

expectations.text: The output states the write will be staged and handed to plm-assistant rather than committed directly via execute_sql
expectations.evidence: 'STAGED ONLY — not executed. plm-assistant is the sole writer, and only after Alvin confirms against a real source email.'

expectations.text: An Asana sync-back comment on the related PD task is mentioned as a follow-on step
expectations.evidence: 'Asana sync-back: [would post to the KDC/One tooling task in SJS Ops once confirmed]'

expectations.text: The skill does not claim to have created a new Asana task
expectations.evidence: No Asana write is claimed; output explicitly states 'No PLM write, no Asana post, and no plm-assistant hand-off happened.'

eval_feedback.overall: Full pass, and it correctly refused to fabricate a source email for the ack — flagged the mismatch and produced an explicitly-labeled illustrative preview instead of pretending to have found real confirmation. That's the right failure mode for a system gating real PLM writes.
